{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":778621,"duration_api_ms":765956,"num_turns":73,"result":"Step 1 complete. The `obpub audit` command now scans `dist/` for privacy leaks via 6 always-on rules + 1 strict-only rule:

- `private-note-title-in-html` / `authored-private-title-mention` (strict-only short-title variant)
- `private-attachment-in-dist`
- `graph-edge-leaks-private` (nodes + edges, accepts `from/to` or `source/target`)
- `frontmatter-allowlist-violation` (scans `data-fm-{key}` markers)
- `obsidian-comment-leak` (`%%...%%` survival)
- `tag-blocklist-leak` (literal-prefix glob expansion, noop when empty)

Audit consumes — never re-derives — privacy verdicts: `runAudit` calls `runCorePipeline` once and feeds `publicSlugs`, `privateNoteTitles`, and the closure-derived private attachment set into the rule engine. `PipelineResult` gained two read-only fields (`privateNoteTitles`, `allAttachments`) to surface what audit needs without leaking classification logic into the CLI. Violation messages redact private titles to a 4-char head + sha256 prefix so CI logs can't leak names.
